pi_items.source: Confirmed via export_result.sh script and verifier expectations.
Confirmed via verifier.py explicit property checks.
Code analysis of Muncie.p04.tmp.hdf shows exactly 61 cross-sections, verifying the >5 minimum threshold.
Confirmed via setup_task.sh which launches the terminal here.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on real-world HEC-RAS data from Muncie, Indiana. The output path (/home/ga/Documents/hec_ras_results/muncie_results.geojson) and required properties (station, peak_wse, peak_flow) accurately reflect the evaluation logic in verifier.py. Analysis of the Muncie HDF5 dataset confirms 61 cross-sections, satisfying the min_features threshold. The project directory claim is accurate. Critically, the task evaluation script (export_result.sh) contains a bug: it looks for cross sections under the legacy path '/Geometry/Cross Sections/River Stations' rather than the modern HEC-RAS 6.6 path ('.../Attributes'). This causes the ground truth script to fail, which verifier.py misinterprets as a 'System Error' and inadvertently awards a 100% score to any agent execution that produces a valid JSON file, bypassing all rigorous validation.
